title: The Hodge Locus
speaker: Gregorio Baldi
abstract: We will survey various recent results around the distribution of the Hodge locus of a (mixed) variation of Hodge structures. Various concrete applications to moduli spaces will also be presented.
tags: Workshop on Special Cycles and Related Topics

proof_stages.label: Hodge-locus framework
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes a smooth quasi-projective base with a polarized pure integral variation of Hodge structure, defines special cycles as Hodge classes in Tannakian tensor constructions, identifies the Hodge locus with points where the connected Mumford-Tate group drops, and recalls the Cattani-Deligne-Kaplan algebraicity theorem.

proof_stages.label: Typical-atypical Hodge-locus philosophy
proof_stages.summary: The positive-dimensional Hodge locus is organized via period maps into typical and atypical components, with the Zilber-Pink-type conjectural picture that maximal atypical components should be finite while the typical locus should be empty or dense.

proof_stages.label: Bézout-type intersection statements
proof_stages.summary: The classical Bézout dimension-count situation is abstracted and the abelian-variety analogue is stated: Barth's nonemptiness theorem is refined to high-degree endomorphism translates having expected dimension, with dense unions of expected components.

proof_stages.label: Atypical-intersection proof mechanism in abelian varieties
proof_stages.summary: Intersections X cap lambda Y in an abelian variety are recast as intersections of X times Y with graphs of endomorphisms, so excess-dimensional components become positive-dimensional atypical Hodge intersections controlled by geometric Zilber-Pink, with equidistribution supplying density and uniform refinements.

proof_stages.label: Shimura Hecke-correspondence theorem
proof_stages.summary: The abelian setup is replaced by a simple locally symmetric or Shimura quotient S = D/Gamma with Hodge-generic subvarieties X and Y, and the main theorem asserts nonempty expected-dimensional intersections with Hecke correspondences outside a bad algebraic locus and density along generic sequences.

proof_stages.label: Ax-Schanuel deformation argument
proof_stages.summary: Nonemptiness in the Shimura setting is proved by lifting X times Y and correspondence graphs to D times D, using Ax-Schanuel and a Goursat-type control of functional-transcendence outputs to rule out atypical alternatives, while typical real intersections deform to rational Hecke ones and then equidistribution gives the expected density conclusions.

examples.name: Hypersurface VHS and Noether-Lefschetz locus
examples.note: Smooth degree-d hypersurfaces give a concrete VHS via primitive middle cohomology, and the surface case in P^3 illustrates the Hodge locus through the classical Noether-Lefschetz locus of extra algebraic curves.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker instantiates the VHS on the hypersurface parameter space, identifies the fiber as primitive cohomology, and specializes it to the Noether-Lefschetz locus for surfaces in P^3

passes.reason: A standard VHS from smooth hypersurfaces and its Noether–Lefschetz locus are used as motivating instances of Hodge loci with extra cycles.

examples.name: Plane curves requiring tensor constructions
examples.note: The speaker notes that H^1 of a plane curve has no direct odd algebraic cycles, so one must pass to tensor constructions such as H^1 tensor H^1 to see Hodge classes.
examples.engagement_reason: plane curves are invoked only to explain why one needs tensor constructions such as H^1 tensor H^1, without carrying out a calculation on a particular curve

passes.reason: Plane curves have no Hodge classes directly in odd H^1, showing that the naive untensored construction would miss the relevant classes.

examples.name: Product-factor pathology for the genericity hypothesis
examples.note: In response to a question, the point-times-factor type construction shows why the dimension inequality and simplicity of the ambient Shimura variety do not by themselves replace the all-generic monodromy assumption.
examples.engagement_reason: the product-factor case is reasoned through as a pathology for genericity, with product Shimura-type examples used to see how dimension and monodromy issues can force unwanted intersections

passes.reason: A product-factor or fiber-type subvariety with one coordinate collapsed is invoked as a pathology showing why a genericity hypothesis is needed.

examples.name: Existential algebraic closedness analogy
examples.note: The Q&A compares the abelian-variety intersections with model-theoretic existential algebraic closedness over the universal cover C^n times A, especially encoding multiplication by n, as a parallel rather than an input to the proof.
examples.engagement_reason: existential algebraic closedness is discussed as an analogy and possible related framework, but no concrete computation or proof is carried out with it
